# Galileo Brain v6 "Onnipotenza" — Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Generate a stratified training dataset (~12,200 examples) that teaches Galileo Brain to directly execute ALL 42 simulator actions without LLM delegation, with 100 linguistic variants per action, context awareness, and adversarial robustness. Then fine-tune the existing v5 model with continual training.

**Architecture:** 4-layer stratified dataset (Replay + Direct Action + Context-Aware + Adversarial) generated by a new Python script `generate-brain-v6.py`. Continual training on Colab A100 using the existing v5 LoRA weights as starting point. Anti-forgetting via replay layer.

**Tech Stack:** Python 3 (dataset generation), Unsloth + QLoRA (training), Qwen3-4B (base model), GGUF q4_k_m (deployment), Ollama (inference)

---

## Simulator Capabilities Reference

### All 42 Actionable Tags (grouped by category)

**Simulation Control (6):**
| # | Action Tag | Description | Example Response |
|---|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | `[AZIONE:play]` | Start simulation | "Simulazione avviata! ▶" |
| 2 | `[AZIONE:pause]` | Pause simulation | "In pausa. ⏸" |
| 3 | `[AZIONE:reset]` | Reset to initial state | "Circuito resettato." |
| 4 | `[AZIONE:clearall]` | Clear breadboard | "Breadboard svuotata!" |
| 5 | `[AZIONE:undo]` | Undo last action | "Annullato!" |
| 6 | `[AZIONE:redo]` | Redo undone action | "Ripetuto!" |

**Compilation & Code (7):**
| # | Action Tag | Description | Example Response |
|---|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| 7 | `[AZIONE:compile]` | Compile Arduino/Scratch code | "Compilazione avviata." |
| 8 | `[AZIONE:openeditor]` | Open code editor panel | "Editor aperto!" |
| 9 | `[AZIONE:closeeditor]` | Close code editor panel | "Editor chiuso." |
| 10 | `[AZIONE:switcheditor:scratch]` | Switch to Blockly mode | "Modalità Scratch attiva! 🧩" |
| 11 | `[AZIONE:switcheditor:arduino]` | Switch to Arduino C++ mode | "Modalità Arduino C++ attiva!" |
| 12 | `[AZIONE:resetcode]` | Reset code to default | "Codice resettato." |
| 13 | `[AZIONE:getcode]` | Retrieve current code | "Ecco il codice attuale." |

**Navigation (5):**
| # | Action Tag | Description | Example Response |
|---|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| 14 | `[AZIONE:loadexp:ID]` | Load experiment | "Esperimento caricato! 📖" |

photo-resistor, diode, mosfet-n, rgb-led, motor-dc, servo,
reed-switch, phototransistor, battery9v, multimeter, lcd16x2,
nano-r4-board, breadboard-half, breadboard-full, wire

```
led, resistor, push-button, buzzer-piezo, capacitor, potentiometer,
photo-resistor, diode, mosfet-n, rgb-led, motor-dc, servo,
reed-switch, phototransistor, lcd16x2
```

### 10 Tabs
```
simulator, manual, video, canvas, editor, taccuini, detective, poe, reverse, review
```

### 69 Experiments (38 Vol1 + 18 Vol2 + 13 Vol3)

### NanoBreakout Pin Map
- Digital: D0/RX, D1/TX, D3, D5, D6, D9, D10, D11, D12, D13
- Analog: A0, A1, A2, A3, A4/SDA, A5/SCL
- NOT available: D2, D4, D7, D8

---

## Dataset Architecture: 4 Strati

### Strato 1 — Replay (~3,000 examples)
**Purpose:** Prevent catastrophic forgetting. Regenerate core routing examples from v5 but with the new direct-action philosophy.

| Category | Count | needs_llm |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| Action commands (14 types × 30 variants) | 420 | false |
| Circuit placement (single + multi) | 400 | false |
| Wiring (pin-to-pin, bus, wing) | 300 | false |
| Navigation (experiments + tabs + volumes) | 300 | false |
| Code questions (Arduino + Scratch) | 400 | true |
| Tutor questions (theory + comparison + debug) | 600 | true |
| Vision requests | 200 | true |
| Teacher requests | 200 | true |
| Off-topic / negation | 180 | false/true |
| **Total** | **~3,000** | |

### Strato 2 — Direct Action (42 × 100 = ~4,200 examples)
**Purpose:** 100 linguistic variants per action tag. The Brain learns to recognize ANY phrasing and produce the correct action with `needs_llm: false`.

Each variant includes:
- Italian formal/informal/slang/dialect
- Typos (30% probability)
- Abbreviations ("resistenza" → "res", "potenziometro" → "pot")
- Emoji/emoticons
- Incomplete sentences
- Questions phrased as commands ("si può avviare?" → play)
- Negative commands ("non fermarlo" → do nothing vs "fermalo" → pause)
- English mixed ("fai il play", "start please")
- Child speech patterns ("quella cosa che fa andare il circuito" → play)

**Per-action variant targets:**

| Action Group | Actions | Variants/action | Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sim control (play/pause/reset/clearall/undo/redo) | 6 | 120 | 720 |
| Compile & code editor (compile/open/close/switch×2/reset/get) | 7 | 100 | 700 |
| Navigation (loadexp/opentab/openvolume/next/prev) | 5 | 100 | 500 |
| Component ops (place/remove/move/setvalue/highlight/highlightpin) | 6 | 110 | 660 |
| Wiring (addwire/removewire) | 2 | 100 | 200 |
| Interaction (interact/measure/diagnose/getstate) | 4 | 100 | 400 |
| UI panels (showbom/showserial/showshortcuts/fullscreen/exitfullscreen) | 5 | 80 | 400 |
| Educational (quiz/youtube/createnotebook) | 3 | 100 | 300 |
| Serial & code (serialwrite/setcode/appendcode) | 3 | 80 | 240 |
| Build mode (setbuildmode) | 1 | 80 | 80 |
| **Total** | **42** | | **~4,200** |

### Strato 3 — Context-Aware (~3,000 examples)
**Purpose:** Same actions but with rich simulator context. The Brain learns to use context to disambiguate and produce smarter responses.

Context format in user message:
```
[CONTESTO]
tab: simulator
esperimento: v3-cap2-esp1
componenti: [led1, resistor1, pushbutton1]
fili: 4
volume_attivo: 3
simulazione: running/paused/stopped
build_mode: passopasso
step_corrente: 3/7
editor_mode: scratch/arduino
codice_presente: true/false
```

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Context-dependent disambiguation | 600 | "compilalo" when on editor tab → compile; when no code → "Scrivi prima il codice!" |
| Multi-action sequences | 500 | "metti un LED, collegalo e avvia" → 3 actions in one |
| Conditional responses | 400 | "il LED non si accende" with LED on circuit → diagnose; without LED → suggest adding |
| Experiment-aware | 500 | "carica il prossimo" → needs context of current experiment to know which is next |
| Step-aware (passo passo) | 300 | "cosa devo fare?" when step 3/7 → nextstep + hint about current step |
| Editor-mode aware | 300 | "compila" when scratch mode → compile scratch; arduino mode → compile arduino |
| Component-state aware | 400 | "togli quello rosso" with 2 LEDs → needs context to identify which one |
| **Total** | **~3,000** | |

### Strato 4 — Adversarial (~2,000 examples)
**Purpose:** Robustness against weird, ambiguous, sgrammaticate, mixed-language, or trap inputs.

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Heavy typos (50%+ error rate) | 300 | "avía la simulazzione", "mtti un leddd" |
| Mixed Italian/English | 200 | "fai il start del circuit", "compila the code" |
| Extremely vague | 300 | "fai quella cosa", "metti il coso", "vai là" |
| Multi-intent in one sentence | 300 | "metti un LED poi avvia e se non funziona dimmi perché" |
| Negations & corrections | 200 | "no aspetta non quello, l'altro", "ho detto pausa non play" |
| Conversational wrap | 200 | "sai che ieri ho fatto un circuito? comunque puoi avviare?" |
| Emoji-only or minimal | 100 | "▶️", "🔴", "❌", "⏸", "💡" |
| Dialect/slang heavy | 200 | "daje metti sta lucina", "ammazza che bel circuito, fallo andà" |
| Trap: looks like action but is question | 200 | "cosa succede se avvio?" (tutor, NOT action) |
| **Total** | **~2,000** | |

### Grand Total: ~12,200 examples

---

## System Prompt v6

The system prompt must be updated to:
1. Include `teacher` intent (missing in v5 system prompt used by 20k generator)
2. Add context fields (simulation state, editor mode, etc.)
3. Clarify direct action philosophy: `needs_llm: false` whenever possible
4. Add build mode and step awareness

---

## Task Breakdown

### Task 1: Create the v6 Generator Script — Foundation

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py`

**Step 1: Write the script skeleton with constants**

Create `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py` with:
- Updated SYSTEM_PROMPT_V6 (includes teacher intent, context fields, direct action rules)
- All 42 action tags as structured data (not just phrase lists)
- All component aliases (expanded from v5's COMPONENT_ALIASES)
- Updated EXPERIMENTS list with all 69 experiments
- Tab aliases (expanded)
- Pin map reference
- Context generator (`random_context_v6()` with all new fields)
- CLI args: `--count`, `--seed`, `--stats`, `--validate`, `--layer` (replay/action/context/adversarial/all)

Key data structure — each action as a dict:
```python
ACTIONS = {
    "play": {
        "tag": "[AZIONE:play]",
        "intent": "action",
        "category": "simulation",
        "responses": ["Simulazione avviata! ▶", "Play! ▶", "Si parte!", ...],
        "phrases": [... 100+ variants ...],
        "entities": [],
        "context_rules": {
            "already_running": "La simulazione è già in corso!",
            "no_components": "Non ci sono componenti. Aggiungi qualcosa prima!",
        }
    },
    ...
}
```

**Step 2: Run skeleton to verify it loads**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --stats`
Expected: Shows action count, component count, no errors

**Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: add Galileo Brain v6 generator skeleton with all 42 actions"
```

---

### Task 2: Implement Strato 1 — Replay Layer (~3,000 examples)

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py`

**Step 1: Write replay generators**

Implement functions:
- `gen_replay_actions(n)` — 14 action types × 30 variants each
- `gen_replay_circuit(n)` — single + multi placement, basic wiring
- `gen_replay_navigation(n)` — loadexp, opentab, openvolume
- `gen_replay_tutor(n)` — theory questions, comparisons, debugging (needs_llm=true)
- `gen_replay_code(n)` — code questions (needs_llm=true)
- `gen_replay_vision(n)` — vision requests (needs_llm=true)
- `gen_replay_teacher(n)` — teacher dashboard requests (needs_llm=true)
- `gen_replay_offtopic(n)` — off-topic, negations

These are similar to v5's generators but with:
- Updated system prompt v6
- `teacher` intent included
- Direct action responses (needs_llm=false where possible)

**Step 2: Generate and validate replay layer**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer replay --count 3000 --seed 42 --validate`
Expected: 3,000 examples, 0 validation errors, balanced intent distribution

**Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: implement Strato 1 replay layer (3000 examples)"
```

---

### Task 3: Implement Strato 2 — Direct Action Layer (~4,200 examples)

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py`

**Step 1: Write the 100-variant phrase generator for each action group**

For each of the 42 actions, generate 80-120 variants using:
- `PHRASE_TEMPLATES[action]` — base templates
- `augment_phrase()` — prefixes, suffixes, case changes
- `apply_typo()` — realistic typos (30% probability)
- `add_slang()` — child slang, abbreviations
- `mix_language()` — Italian/English mixing
- `expand_alias()` — component aliases for entity-bearing actions

Implement specialized generators:
- `gen_direct_sim_control(n)` — play/pause/reset/clearall/undo/redo
- `gen_direct_compile_editor(n)` — compile/open/close/switch/reset/get
- `gen_direct_navigation(n)` — loadexp/opentab/openvolume/next/prev
- `gen_direct_component_ops(n)` — place/remove/move/setvalue/highlight
- `gen_direct_wiring(n)` — addwire/removewire with pin specifics
- `gen_direct_interaction(n)` — interact/measure/diagnose/getstate
- `gen_direct_ui_panels(n)` — showbom/showserial/showshortcuts/fullscreen
- `gen_direct_educational(n)` — quiz/youtube/createnotebook
- `gen_direct_serial_code(n)` — serialwrite/setcode/appendcode
- `gen_direct_buildmode(n)` — setbuildmode

Key: ALL these examples must have `needs_llm: false` with a complete `response` string.

**Step 2: Generate and validate direct action layer**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer action --count 4200 --seed 42 --validate`
Expected: ~4,200 examples, all needs_llm=false, all actions covered

**Step 3: Verify coverage — every action tag appears ≥80 times**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer action --count 4200 --seed 42 --stats`
Expected: 42/42 actions covered, min 80 variants each

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: implement Strato 2 direct action layer (4200 examples, 42 actions × 100)"
```

---

### Task 4: Implement Strato 3 — Context-Aware Layer (~3,000 examples)

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py`

**Step 1: Implement rich context generator**

```python
def random_context_v6(
    tab=None, comps=None, vol=None, exp=None,
    wires=None, step=None, sim_state=None,
    editor_mode=None, code_present=None, build_mode=None
):
    """Generate rich simulator context for training examples."""
```

**Step 2: Write context-aware generators**

- `gen_context_disambiguation(n)` — same phrase, different action based on context
- `gen_context_multi_action(n)` — "metti un LED, collegalo e avvia" → 3 sequential actions
- `gen_context_conditional(n)` — response changes based on circuit state
- `gen_context_experiment_aware(n)` — "carica il prossimo" needs current experiment
- `gen_context_step_aware(n)` — passo passo context changes response
- `gen_context_editor_aware(n)` — editor mode affects compile/switch behavior
- `gen_context_component_state(n)` — "togli quello rosso" needs component list

**Step 3: Generate and validate**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer context --count 3000 --seed 42 --validate`
Expected: 3,000 examples with rich context, varied actions

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: implement Strato 3 context-aware layer (3000 examples)"
```

---

### Task 5: Implement Strato 4 — Adversarial Layer (~2,000 examples)

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/generate-brain-v6.py`

**Step 1: Write adversarial generators**

- `gen_adversarial_typos(n)` — 50%+ typo rate
- `gen_adversarial_mixed_lang(n)` — Italian/English hybrid
- `gen_adversarial_vague(n)` — "fai quella cosa", "metti il coso"
- `gen_adversarial_multi_intent(n)` — multiple intents in one sentence
- `gen_adversarial_negation(n)` — corrections, negations
- `gen_adversarial_conversational(n)` — buried intent in conversation
- `gen_adversarial_emoji(n)` — emoji-only or minimal text
- `gen_adversarial_dialect(n)` — Roman/Milanese/Southern Italian dialect
- `gen_adversarial_traps(n)` — looks like action but is question (tutor intent)

**Step 2: Generate and validate**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer adversarial --count 2000 --seed 42 --validate`
Expected: 2,000 examples, high linguistic variety

**Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/generate-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: implement Strato 4 adversarial layer (2000 examples)"
```

### Task 6: Generate Full Dataset + Eval Set

**Files:**
- Create: `datasets/output/galileo-brain-v6-onnipotenza.jsonl`
- Create: `datasets/output/galileo-brain-v6-eval.jsonl`

**Step 1: Generate full training dataset**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --layer all --seed 42`
Expected: ~12,200 examples in `datasets/output/galileo-brain-v6-onnipotenza.jsonl`

**Step 2: Generate eval dataset**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --eval --count 300 --seed 99`
Expected: ~300 eval examples (stratified: 5 per action + context + adversarial)

**Step 3: Run full validation**

Run: `python3 scripts/generate-brain-v6.py --validate --stats`
Expected output:
- 0 JSON parse errors
- 42/42 action tags covered
- All 7 intents represented
- Intent distribution balanced
- Entity coverage ≥90% of components

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add datasets/output/galileo-brain-v6-onnipotenza.jsonl datasets/output/galileo-brain-v6-eval.jsonl
git commit -m "feat: generate Galileo Brain v6 dataset (12200 train + 300 eval)"
```

---

### Task 7: Update Colab Notebook for Continual Training

**Files:**
- Create: `notebooks/galileo-brain-finetune-v6.ipynb`

**Step 1: Write the v6 notebook**

Key differences from v5 notebook:
- **Cell 2**: Load model from v5 LoRA checkpoint (not base Qwen3-4B)
  ```python
  model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
      model_name = "galileo-brain-v5/checkpoint-best",  # or merged model
      max_seq_length = 2048,
      load_in_4bit = True,
  )
  ```
- **Cell 6**: Training args tuned for continual training:
  - Lower learning rate: `5e-5` (not `2e-4` — we're refining, not learning from scratch)
  - Fewer epochs: `2` (not `3` — smaller dataset, risk of overfitting)
  - Warmup ratio: `0.1` (10% — more gentle warm-in)
  - Same eval every 200 steps
- **Cell 8**: Expanded test suite (42 action tests + context tests + adversarial tests)
- **Cell 9**: Eval on 300 examples with per-action breakdown

**Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add notebooks/galileo-brain-finetune-v6.ipynb
git commit -m "feat: add v6 continual training notebook"
```

---

### Task 8: Training on Colab (MANUAL)

**Not automated — user executes on Colab A100:**

1. Upload notebook + 2 JSONL files
2. Execute cells 1-10
3. Download GGUF + Modelfile
4. Report metrics:
   - Target: eval intent accuracy ≥ 95%
   - Target: 0 parse errors
   - Target: all 42 actions covered in eval
   - Target: adversarial accuracy ≥ 85%

---

### Task 9: Ollama Local Test

**Step 1: Create Ollama model**

```bash
cd ~/Downloads  # or wherever GGUF was downloaded
ollama create galileo-brain-v6 -f Modelfile
```

**Step 2: Run 42-action smoke test**

```bash
python3 scripts/test-brain-v6.py --all-actions
```

Expected: 42/42 actions correctly routed

**Step 3: Run adversarial test**

```bash
python3 scripts/test-brain-v6.py --adversarial
```

Expected: ≥85% accuracy on adversarial inputs

**Step 4: Commit test script**

```bash
git add scripts/test-brain-v6.py
git commit -m "feat: add v6 test suite (42 actions + adversarial)"
```

---

### Task 10: Shadow Mode Integration in Nanobot

**Files:**
- Modify: `nanobot/server.py` — update shadow mode to use v6 model name
- Modify: `nanobot/server.py` — add v6 metrics logging

**Step 1: Update shadow mode config**

In `server.py`, update the Ollama model name:
```python
BRAIN_MODEL = os.getenv("BRAIN_MODEL", "galileo-brain-v6")
```

**Step 2: Deploy to Render**

```bash
cd nanobot && git add -A && git commit -m "feat: update brain model to v6" && git push render main
```

**Step 3: Verify shadow mode working**

Check Render logs for brain predictions alongside LLM responses.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat: integrate Galileo Brain v6 shadow mode"
```

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target | Method |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Eval intent accuracy | ≥ 95% | Cell 9 eval suite |
| Eval parse errors | 0 | Cell 9 eval suite |
| Action coverage | 42/42 | Smoke test |
| Adversarial accuracy | ≥ 85% | Adversarial test |
| Context-aware accuracy | ≥ 90% | Context test |
| Training loss | < 0.05 | Colab training |
| GGUF size | ~2.5 GB | Post-export |
| Inference latency | < 200ms | Ollama local test |

## Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Catastrophic forgetting | Strato 1 replay (3,000 examples of core routing) |
| Overfitting on small dataset | Lower LR (5e-5), fewer epochs (2), early stopping |
| Action tag hallucination | Structured `ACTIONS` dict validates all tags at generation time |
| Context format mismatch | Context generator mirrors exact nanobot context format |
| Adversarial too hard | Gradual difficulty: 30% mild, 40% medium, 30% extreme |
